sources.label: Investing.com - Vecima Networks Q3 2026 earnings call transcript (May 14, 2026)
Light Reading - Vecima secures more vCMTS wins, ups revenue guidance
Vecima Networks - investor news releases page

verdicts.rankRationale: No material news since June 6; the thesis-relevant catalysts (Charter DOCSIS 4.0 multi-year agreement, raised CY2026 guide of +22.5-30% revenue / +74-85% adj. EBITDA, Cox and Witke vCMTS wins) all date from mid-May and were already embedded in the June 8 hold. Today's -1.76% on a $0.24B illiquid TSX small cap is flow noise, not a setup change, so the latent-node case for an underfollowed broadband-access bottleneck stays exactly where it was.
verdicts.bull: vCMTS is a three-vendor global market and Vecima has Charter, Cox and European wins with management citing PO-backed visibility into CY2026 volume ramps; at $0.24B and minimal coverage, residual alpha from the qualification-to-deployment transition is still real.
verdicts.neutral: Q3 revenue of CAD64.8M was only +1.3% y/y and down 12.1% sequentially with an EPS miss, so the raised CY2026 guide is still a promise, not a print; +23.75% YTD has already paid for part of the rerating.
verdicts.bear: Customer concentration in a handful of North American MSOs means any capex deferral by Charter or Cox guts the deployment ramp, and lumpy quarterly revenue plus thin liquidity makes drawdowns violent for a name this small.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q4 FY2026 results (and first CY2026 quarters) confirming the 22.5-30% revenue ramp and initial vCMTS deployment revenue at Charter/Cox.
verdicts.primaryRisk: MSO capex slippage or DOCSIS 4.0 deployment delays pushing the guided CY2026 ramp to the right, breaking the guidance-driven rerating.
